rulings.Case Title: ELEN MEDER ET AL VS. COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, HUMAN SERVICES AGENCY,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 1, PETITIONERS ELEN MEDER AND WALTER MEDER'S Motion To Seal Record. Petitioners' unopposed motion to seal record is granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Petitioners are required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the foregoing findings and states that the motion was not opposed, and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SHOUN THAO VS. CASTILLO TRUCKING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Marco Castillo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS SHOUN THAO AND ANGELA LEE'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Defendant Marco Castillo. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' motion is granted. Defendant Castillo is ordered to appear for deposition on August 25, 2020. Plaintiffs shall serve Notice of Deposition pursuant to CCP 2025.270. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $345.10 and shall be paid no later than August 25, 2020. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jskrmpotic@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JULIO SALAZAR ET AL VS. MAGIC GOURMET TRADING COMPANY INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Magic Gourmet Trading Inc Fung Ping Chen Ng Willy Ng And Guanmao Chens Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Good Faith Settlement Determination Declaration Of Scott S Blackstone And Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 5, Defendants Magic Gourmet Trading Inc, Fung Ping Chen Ng, Willy Ng, and Guanmao Chen's Motion For A Good Faith Settlement Determination. Defendants Magic Gourmet Trading, Inc., Fung Ping Chen Ng, Willy Ng, and Guanmao Chen's motion to determine good faith settlement is granted. The party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden of proof on that issue (Code Civ. Proc. § 877.6(d)). In this case, objector Koi Palace fails to meet its burden and the settlors have provided substantial evidence that the settlement is in the ballpark. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488; Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348, 1350 at n.6 [substantial evidence necessary to find that settlement is in good faith].) In Tech-Bilt, Inc., 38 Cal.3d at 499, the California Supreme Court held the following factors are relevant in evaluating the good faith of a settlement: 1) a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability; 2) the amount paid in settlement; 3) the allocation of settlement proceeds among plaintiffs; 4) a recognition that a settlor should pay less in settlement than he would if he were found liable after a trial; 5) the financial conditions of the settling defendants, as well as the existence of collusion, fraud, or tortious conduct aimed to injure the interests of non-settling defendants; and 6) defendant's settlement figure must not be grossly disproportionate to what a reasonable person, at the time of the settlement, would estimate the settling defendant's liability to be. Here, Koi Palace asserts that plaintiff incurred $811,872 in past medical damages and could recover $5 million. (Opposition, 4:22-25). The $3.9 million settlement is in good faith given these damages, settlors' proportionate fault (which is high), and the uncertainty regarding plaintiff's life expectancy. The allocation to plaintiff's wife of $400,000 is also reasonable. There is no evidence of collusion and settlors are paying a large percentage of their available insurance. The settlement is in good faith given the totality of the circumstances. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the settlors is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH KENNARD VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Mtn Hearing To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 6, PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD'S Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award. Plaintiff Kennard's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied as untimely. A party may "file and serve a petition to vacate that award 'not later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award.'" (Santa Monica College Faculty Ass'n v. Santa Monica Community College Dist. (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 538, 544; see also Eternity Invs., Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 739, 745 ["If one wishes to have an award vacated or corrected he must act within one hundred days of service of the award or be precluded from attacking the award." (internal brackets and quotations omitted); Code of Civil Procedure, § 1288].) Here, the arbitrator served the award by both regular mail and email on July 29, 2019. (Award attached to Petition/Complaint.) The 100-day period elapsed on November 6, 2019, the day plaintiff filed his Petition/Complaint. Assuming that the "petition to vacate" was timely filed, plaintiff failed to serve it within the 100-day period. Plaintiff mailed the petition to defendant on November 12, 2019, which was outside the 100-day period. (Smyth Dec. Ex. D; proofs of service entered on January 30, 2020). Service was effectuated on December 23, 2020, when defendant acknowledged receipt and service. Therefore, under any conceivable calculation, plaintiff failed to file and serve the petition within the 100-day period. Because plaintiff failed to comply with these jurisdictional timing requirements, the court cannot address the merits of plaintiff's arguments and plaintiff cannot obtain relief. (See Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal. App. 4th 1199, 1211 [failure to file and serve petition within the 100-day period is fatal to the petition to vacate].) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Kaiser is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DENEEN MYISHA JONES VS. MARK DENES BIBO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Entry Of Default And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Alexander Macdougall
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 9, DEFENDANT MARK BIBO'S Motion To Set Aside Request For Entry Of Default And Entry Of Default And Request For Sanctions. Defendant's motion to set aside the default entered against him on April 2, 2020 is granted. "Aside from statute, courts have an inherent power to correct judgments where there has been a clerical error by the clerk . . . There is no time limit within which this correction need be made . . . [T]he power to amend . . . extends . . . to cases where some provision of . . . an order or judgment as made or rendered was due to the inadvertence or mistake of the court and may therefore properly be treated as a clerical misprision." (Morgan v. State Bd. of Equalization (1949) 89 Cal.App.2d 674, 677.) Pursuant to the COVID-19 emergency rules and court holidays, Defendant's responsive pleading was not yet due on April 2, and it was an error for default to be entered. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Oasis Grill Llc And Mohammad Zughaiyer_S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 5, 2020, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS OASIS GRILL, LLC AND MOHAMMAD ZUGHAIYER'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As a threshold issue, the initial burden rests with the propounding party setting forth justification in a declaration of necessity. 964 discovery requests may be excessive, the limitation sought by Defendant may be unduly restrictive given the allegations of the complaint, cross-complaint and the affirmative defenses to each. Further information is required for the court to address to scope of a protective order. The responses served by plaintiffs are not at issue. It is also noted that meet and confer efforts to date have not been effective to resolve the issues presented by the motion, and the parties are ordered to make further attempts at informal resolution. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jskrmpotic@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
